example_from_field: Samir Sood, Head of Change Management at Accenture India, led the change management programme for India's largest-ever ERP implementation at a major PSU — 1,20,000 employees across 200 locations. His structured change champion network (800 sessions) reduced post-go-live productivity dip by 60% versus industry average. He has trained over 1,000 change management practitioners in India, building the country's change management professional community.

title: Cyber Law Specialist
description: Cyber Law Specialists handle legal matters related to digital technology — cybercrime prosecution, data privacy compliance under DPDPA 2023, IT Act matters, cryptocurrency regulation, social media law, and e-commerce legal compliance. India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 — India's first comprehensive data protection law — has created unprecedented demand for cyber law expertise. India recorded 13 lakh cybercrime complaints in 2022 (NCRB), creating a prosecution workload requiring specialised legal expertise. CERT-In incident notifications, SEBI cybersecurity frameworks, RBI digital banking guidelines, and MeitY's IT Intermediary Rules all require compliance expertise that is in extremely short supply.
personality_traits: You are analytically rigorous in interpreting complex technology legislation
You are curious about both the legal and technical dimensions of digital systems
You are detail-oriented in building digital compliance and risk management frameworks
You stay current with rapidly evolving Indian and international cyber law
You have strong communication skills for client advisories and regulatory submissions
You are ethically and privacy-conscious in handling digital evidence
required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream (10+2); LLB + technology background; BSc Computer Science + LLB is the ideal combination
entrance_exams: CLAT for NLUs — primary pathway
AILET for NLU Delhi
State law school entrance exams
CII Cyber Law training programme selection
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 → LLB 5-year integrated or BA + LLB 3-year → LLM in Cyber Law or IT Law at NALSAR, NLU Delhi, or Symbiosis → Internship at a cyber law firm or IT company's legal team → Join as junior cyber law associate → Build expertise in DPDPA compliance, IT Act, and cybercrime prosecution → Advance to Senior Cyber Law Counsel and Partner.
course_fee_inr: INR 30,000 – 3,00,000 per year (LLB); INR 50,000 – 3,00,000 for LLM Cyber Law
career_growth_path: Junior Cyber Law Associate → Cyber Law Counsel → Senior Counsel → Partner → Managing Partner / Head of Digital Law Practice

work_environment.places_of_work: Cyber law firms, IT company legal departments, startup legal teams, CERT-In, MeitY advisory, cybercrime courts, SEBI enforcement division, international law firms with India offices
work_environment.work_environment: Office-based legal practice. Involves reading technology law, drafting data protection policies, advising on cyber incident response, appearing before Adjudicating Officers under the IT Act, and preparing DPDPA compliance frameworks.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: High — can start an independent cyber law practice or data privacy consulting firm for startups and SMEs.

example_from_field: Pavan Duggal, Advocate at the Supreme Court of India and India's foremost cyber law expert, has practised Indian cyber law since the IT Act 2000's enactment. He has handled over 10,000 cyber cases, trains police cybercrime cells across India, and has been cited by the government's IT policy committees. His 25-year career documenting India's evolving cyber jurisprudence through 100+ publications makes him India's most authoritative voice on digital legal regulation.

title: Mediator / ADR Specialist
description: Mediators and Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) Specialists help disputing parties — in commercial, family, labour, and community contexts — reach mutually acceptable settlements outside courts. India's commercial court system has 4.5+ crore pending cases, making ADR critically important. The Mediation Act 2023 and Arbitration Act amendments have significantly expanded mediation's role. SEBI, IRDAI, RERA, and banks use mediation for financial disputes. Lok Adalat resolved over 1 crore cases in 2022 alone. India's ADR infrastructure — ICADR (International Centre for Alternative Dispute Resolution), IIAM (Indian Institute of Arbitration and Mediation), and NALSA Lok Adalats — creates diverse career pathways.
personality_traits: You are empathetic and skilled at understanding all parties' underlying interests
You are calm and patient in managing high-conflict situations
You are impartial and maintain neutrality under pressure
You are an excellent active listener and facilitator
You are creative in generating mutually acceptable resolution options
You have strong ethical standards and maintain strict confidentiality
required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream (10+2); LLB is the most common foundation for ADR specialists
entrance_exams: CLAT for NLUs — primary pathway
State Bar Council LLB entrance
NALSA mediator empanelment process
ICADR mediator training and certification
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 → LLB (5-year BA LLB or 3-year LLB after graduation) → Enrol as advocate → Attend ICADR or NALSA 40-hour basic mediator training → Co-mediate under a senior mediator → Obtain certification from ICADR or IIAM → Empanel with commercial courts, RERA, or consumer forums → Build specialist ADR practice.
course_fee_inr: INR 30,000 – 3,00,000 per year (LLB); INR 10,000 – 50,000 for mediation certification training
career_growth_path: Trainee Mediator → Junior Mediator → Mediator → Senior Mediator → Lead Mediator / ADR Head → ADR Tribunal Member

work_environment.places_of_work: ICADR centres, commercial courts, RERA dispute resolution forums, SEBI and IRDAI ombudsman offices, corporate ADR cells, law firms with ADR practice, NALSA Lok Adalats
work_environment.work_environment: Mediation room or office. Involves joint sessions with disputing parties, private caucuses, drafting mediation agreements, and maintaining confidential case files. Low travel; high interpersonal intensity with emotionally charged clients.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: High — can start an independent mediation centre or ADR consulting firm for commercial and family disputes.

example_from_field: Justice M. Jagannadha Rao, former Chairman of the Law Commission of India, shaped India's modern arbitration and mediation framework. His recommendations led to the Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996 and subsequent amendments transforming commercial dispute resolution in India. His advocacy for mediation at the Supreme Court level helped make mediation a mainstream mechanism, reducing commercial court pendency significantly and saving thousands of crores in litigation costs for Indian businesses.

title: Public Policy Researcher
description: Public Policy Researchers study government programmes, regulatory frameworks, and policy interventions — generating evidence-based analyses and recommendations to improve governance and public service delivery. India's complex policy landscape — MGNREGS (world's largest employment guarantee), PM Awas Yojana, Digital India, Ayushman Bharat, Smart Cities — generates enormous demand for rigorous policy evaluation. NITI Aayog, CPR, CSDS, J-PAL South Asia, and international organisations (World Bank, UNDP, ADB India) all employ policy researchers. India's 75-year democratic system with 3-tier governance creates an unparalleled laboratory for comparative policy research.
personality_traits: You are intellectually curious about governance, institutions, and policy outcomes
You are analytically rigorous using quantitative and qualitative research methods
You are passionate about evidence-based policy and improving government effectiveness
You write clearly for academic audiences and government decision-makers
You are skilled at distilling complex analysis into actionable recommendations
You are collaborative in multi-stakeholder research environments
required_stream_10_plus_2: Humanities, Economics, or Social Sciences preferred; any stream accepted
entrance_exams: JNU/TISS/Jamia entrance for social science MA/MPhil/PhD
UPSC IAS (for government policy career)
ICSSR Junior Research Fellowship examination
NITI Aayog Young Professional programme selection
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 (Humanities/Social Sciences preferred) → BA in Economics, Political Science, Sociology, or Public Administration (3 years) → MA in Public Policy or Social Science (2 years) → Internship at NITI Aayog, CPR, or J-PAL → PhD for senior positions → Join a think tank, government advisory body, or international development organisation → Advance to Senior Policy Researcher and Research Director.
course_fee_inr: INR 5,000 – 80,000 per year (BA/MA at government universities); INR 5,00,000 – 15,00,000 for IIM/ISB Public Policy programmes
career_growth_path: Research Assistant → Policy Analyst → Senior Policy Analyst → Policy Research Manager → Senior Research Fellow → Director of Policy Research

work_environment.places_of_work: NITI Aayog, CPR, CSDS, TERI, J-PAL South Asia, World Bank India, UNDP India, CII/FICCI policy divisions, Brookings India, government research cells
work_environment.work_environment: Office and field research environment. Involves designing policy evaluations, collecting data, building economic models, drafting policy briefs, and presenting findings to government committees and media.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Moderate — can start a policy consulting firm for government departments, international agencies, and NGOs.

Her policy briefs presented to the Ministry of Finance and NITI Aayog influenced how India's national welfare programmes are designed and evaluated. She is widely cited as the generation-defining voice for evidence-based policy research in India, demonstrating how rigorous research can shape INR lakh crore public programmes.

title: Industrial Psychologist
description: Industrial and Organisational (I/O) Psychologists apply psychological principles to workplace environments — improving employee selection, leadership development, organisational culture, team performance, and employee wellbeing. India's large corporate sector — IT services giants (TCS, Infosys, Wipro employing 500,000+ professionals each), manufacturing conglomerates, and PSEs — increasingly invests in I/O psychology to reduce attrition and build high-performance cultures. NIMHANS's industrial mental health programme, NHRDN (National HRD Network), and India's post-COVID workplace mental health awareness have elevated the discipline's strategic importance. India's Great Resignation wave and growing awareness of burnout in IT and consulting sectors have made I/O psychology a board-level people science concern.
personality_traits: You are deeply curious about human behaviour in organisational contexts
You are analytically rigorous in psychometric assessment design and validation
You are empathetic and skilled at helping both individuals and organisations improve
You are evidence-based in applying psychology research to practical workplace challenges
You are ethically grounded in maintaining confidentiality and assessment fairness
You communicate psychological insights clearly to business leaders who value measurable ROI
required_stream_10_plus_2: Humanities/Social Sciences preferred; BA/BSc Psychology + MA/MSc Organisational Psychology
entrance_exams: State university entrance for BSc Psychology
JNU/TISS/Jamia MA Psychology entrance
UGC-NET Psychology (for research and teaching positions)
XLRI Jamshedpur HRM entrance (alternative senior pathway)
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 (Humanities preferred) → BA/BSc Psychology (3 years) → MA/MSc Industrial and Organisational Psychology at TISS, Delhi University, or Hyderabad University (2 years) → Internship in an HR consulting firm or corporate talent management team → Join as I/O Psychology Consultant or Talent Analyst → Advance to Head of Organisational Development and Chief People Science Officer.
course_fee_inr: INR 5,000 – 80,000 per year (BA/BSc Psychology at government university); INR 30,000 – 3,00,000 for MA/MSc I/O Psychology
career_growth_path: I/O Psychology Intern → OD Analyst → Senior OD Consultant → Head of People Analytics → Chief People Science Officer

work_environment.places_of_work: Corporate HR and OD departments, HR consulting firms (Aon Hewitt, Mercer, Korn Ferry India), NIMHANS, IT company talent management teams, management consulting firms, executive coaching practices
work_environment.work_environment: Office and client-site. Involves psychometric assessments, leadership workshops, employee engagement survey analysis, senior leader coaching, and designing talent development programmes.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: High — can start an I/O psychology consulting practice offering assessment, coaching, and OD services to Indian corporations.

His talent assessment centre using validated psychometric tools identified and developed hundreds of senior leaders. His HRM doctoral research and practical application of I/O psychology principles to a 188,000-employee conglomerate makes him India's most distinguished applied organisational psychologist.

title: Orthotics and Prosthetics Technician
description: Orthotics and Prosthetics Technicians fabricate, fit, and repair orthotic devices (braces, splints) and prosthetic devices (artificial limbs) for persons with physical disabilities. India has 2.68 crore persons with locomotor disabilities and 70,000+ new amputees annually due to trauma and diabetes. The Jaipur Foot — developed by BMVSS — is the world's most widely used below-knee prosthesis, an iconic Indian contribution to global rehabilitation technology. ALIMCO (Artificial Limbs Manufacturing Corporation of India) manufactures and distributes assistive devices across India. RCI (Rehabilitation Council of India) mandates registration for P&O technicians, and the sector faces critical shortage of trained professionals nationwide.
personality_traits: You have good manual dexterity and precision craftsmanship skills
You are empathetic and patient with persons with disabilities during fitting sessions
You are technically skilled working with plastics, fibres, carbon composites, and metals
You are detail-oriented in replicating correct anatomical alignment and prosthetic fit
You enjoy the combination of technical fabrication and patient care
You are creative in solving individual patient-specific functional challenges
required_stream_10_plus_2: Science stream with PCB or PCM; BSc in Prosthetics and Orthotics (BPO) or Diploma in P&O
entrance_exams: State Allied Health Sciences entrance for BSc Prosthetics and Orthotics
AIIMS paramedical entrance
ALIMCO training programme selection
RCI-approved P&O institute entrance
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 PCB/PCM → BSc Prosthetics and Orthotics — BPO (3 years) at RCI-approved institute → Clinical training at a rehabilitation hospital or ALIMCO centre → Register with RCI → Join ALIMCO, BMVSS, or a rehabilitation hospital P&O department → Advance to Senior Prosthetist/Orthotist and Head of Rehabilitation Engineering.
course_fee_inr: INR 20,000 – 1,50,000 per year (BSc Prosthetics and Orthotics)
career_growth_path: P&O Trainee → Junior Prosthetist/Orthotist → Prosthetist/Orthotist → Senior P&O Specialist → Head of Rehabilitation Engineering

work_environment.places_of_work: ALIMCO Kanpur, BMVSS (Jaipur Foot), government hospitals' P&O departments, NGO rehabilitation centres, private prosthetics clinics, district disability camps
work_environment.work_environment: P&O workshop and clinical fitting room. Involves measuring residual limbs, fabricating devices from thermoplastic and composite materials, fitting and aligning prostheses, and conducting gait training with rehabilitated patients.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Moderate — can start a private P&O clinic or rehabilitation technology enterprise.

example_from_field: D.R. Mehta, founder of BMVSS Jaipur (Jaipur Foot), transformed the global prosthetics landscape by making the Jaipur Foot available free to amputees in 26 countries. His P&O technicians have fitted over 1.4 million Jaipur Feet — the world's largest prosthetic limb distribution programme. The Jaipur Foot, designed specifically for India's uneven terrain and barefoot conditions, is cited by MIT Media Lab as one of the top 10 innovations for developing countries.

title: Ceramics Artist / Potter
description: Ceramics Artists and Potters create functional and decorative objects from clay using wheel throwing, hand building, casting, and 3D printing — fired in kilns to create durable ceramic products. India has one of the world's richest pottery traditions — Khurja blue pottery (UP), Longpi black pottery (Manipur), Nilufar red terracotta (Rajasthan), and Kutch craft to contemporary studio ceramics. India's INR 12,000 crore handicrafts export sector includes significant ceramic exports. PM Vishwakarma scheme (INR 3 lakh credit) specifically supports traditional potters. Contemporary studio ceramics combining Indian craft traditions with modern aesthetics is a growing entrepreneurial creative sector.
personality_traits: You are highly creative with strong spatial and tactile intelligence
You are patient and meticulous in mastering ceramic forming and kiln firing techniques
You have an eye for form, texture, surface treatment, and glazing aesthetics
You are physically engaged and enjoy hands-on craft practice
You are entrepreneurially minded about ceramic product development and marketing
You are culturally aware of India's diverse pottery traditions
required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream (10+2); Fine Arts background preferred; craft talent is the primary requirement
entrance_exams: NID entrance exam (for Ceramic and Glass Design)
NIFT entrance (for craft design pathway)
Vishwabharati University Shantiniketan (Kala Bhavana) entrance
State art school entrance for BFA in Ceramics
educational_pathway: Complete 10+2 (Fine Arts preferred) → BFA in Ceramics at NID, MSU Baroda, or government art college (4 years) → MFA Ceramics for research and teaching → Apprenticeship with a traditional pottery community or studio ceramist → Establish a personal studio → Exhibit at craft fairs and international exhibitions → Build ceramic brand through direct sales and online platforms.
course_fee_inr: INR 5,000 – 80,000 per year (BFA at government art colleges); INR 50,000 – 2,00,000 at NID
career_growth_path: Pottery Apprentice → Ceramics Artist → Senior Ceramics Artist → Established Studio Artist → Gallery Owner / Ceramics Brand Founder

work_environment.places_of_work: Personal ceramic studio, pottery cooperatives, art colleges (faculty), craft development centres (DC Handicrafts), ceramic design companies, craft export houses, galleries
work_environment.work_environment: Clay studio — involves wedging clay, throwing on wheels, hand building, applying glazes, loading kilns, and monitoring firing cycles. Physically demanding with clay dust exposure. Creatively fulfilling solitary and collaborative craft practice.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Very high — ceramics artists sell through exhibitions, Etsy, and Instagram, and can build sustainable creative enterprises from a home or community studio.

Lalit Kala Akademi fellowship for ceramics artists
